notifications sometimes don't get delivered until the app is focused
notes:
[stab] Disabled macOS App Nap to address burst-on-focus.

Root cause: the live WebSocket runs in the bun process (src/bun/index.ts:785), not the renderer. The mainview MattermostWebSocketClient is dead code (nothing imports it). When the app is backgrounded, macOS App Nap suspends the bun process timers, deferring bun-to-mainview forwarding of WS events until focus returns, producing a burst of notifications on focus.

Fix:
- scripts/disable-app-nap.ts sets NSAppNapDisabled=true on built app bundles via /usr/libexec/PlistBuddy (idempotent). Unit-tested against a temp bundle (3 tests).
- Wired as top-level scripts.postWrap in electrobun.config.ts. Verified to fire during a packaged build: the canary build log shows the postWrap script running and the freshly-built bundle has NSAppNapDisabled=true.

Gates: typecheck clean, bun test 75/75.

PENDING: manual runtime verification. Run a packaged build, background the app, send messages, and confirm prompt delivery instead of a burst. If App-Nap disable proves insufficient (for example fully-occluded-window WKWebView timer throttling), the next step is an NSProcessInfo beginActivity runtime assertion from the bun process.
Completed the renderer->bun log bridge so the full notification pipeline lands in ONE log file for a packaged (backgrounded) run.

DONE this session:
- New src/mainview/app/rendererLog.ts (pure rendererLogVia(send,tag,args,now) -> {line}) + unit test (3 tests). Kept import-free of heavy frameworks so its test doesn't load the editor/view runtime (same lesson as the mention-helper extraction).
- Wired end-to-end: bun handles bun.messages.rendererLog (appendLogLine verbatim, try/catch); rpc.ts exports rendererLog(tag,...args) bound to electrobun.rpc.send.rendererLog; replaced the 8 renderer console.log('[Renderer]'/'[Notification]') call sites in rpc.ts + useMainViewEvents.ts with rendererLog(...). Dropped performance.now() (process-relative, not comparable across processes) in favor of the ISO Date.now() that formatLogLine already emits.
- Removed dead code in src/bun/index.ts: unused logFile const + now-unused join/LOG_FILENAME imports.

VERIFIED:
- typecheck clean; bun test 82/82 (was 75: +4 logger, +3 rendererLog).
- electrobun build succeeds.
- Ran the freshly-built dev bundle; confirmed the tee writes ~/Library/Logs/antimatter.ericschmar.dev/<channel>/antimatter.log with [session]/[log] [RPC]/[log] [WS] lines and ISO timestamps. Renderer [renderer]/[Notification] lines are appended verbatim (no double-tag) once a post event flows.

CLARIFICATION (not a regression): electrobun only runs scripts.postWrap for canary/stable builds (cli/index.ts line 3738 'if (buildEnvironment !== "dev")' gates the wrap stage that calls runHook('postWrap')). So the dev bundle correctly lacks NSAppNapDisabled; the canary bundle has NSAppNapDisabled=true (verified). App-Nap-disable fix is intact for the env that matters.

PENDING (needs a human at the keyboard): build canary (bun run build:canary), launch the bundle, background the app, have someone send you a message, then inspect the log file. If [WS] Message received timestamps arrive within ~1s of the server send and [log] [Notification] Fired by bun follows promptly -> App-Nap disable is sufficient. If [WS] timestamps cluster at refocus -> still throttled; fall back to an NSProcessInfo beginActivity runtime assertion from the bun process. Log path + grep: tail -f ~/Library/Logs/antimatter.ericschmar.dev/canary/antimatter.log | grep -E 'WS|RPC|Notification|session'.

title: returning to app shows message timeline slightly scrolled up when it should be at the very end
close_reason: MessageTimeline had no handler for the app regaining focus/visibility, so the scroll-to-end pinned by useLayoutEffect/ResizeObserver was never re-asserted on return (messages arriving while hidden got scrolled against stale WKWebView geometry; the 96px fuzzy threshold left any sub-threshold offset uncorrected). Added a visibilitychange + window focus listener that re-snaps to the exact bottom, only when isAtEndRef is true so intentional scroll-up is preserved. typecheck clean, 69 tests pass, build clean. Manual scroll verification in-app still recommended.

title: new messages in timeline cause reactions to disappear and reappear
notes:
ROOT CAUSE: Reactions live in state.posts[id].metadata.reactions (loaded via loadPostReactions), but selectedChannelHistory.posts never carries them. Every incoming WebSocket post calls mutateSelectedChannelHistory(addPostToHistory), creating a new selectedChannelHistory reference, which re-triggered the channel-history sync effect. That effect wholesale-replaced state.posts with the reaction-less history posts (reactions disappear), then async-refetched reactions for ALL posts (reactions reappear) — a visible flicker on every new message.

FIX: Added pure helper applyChannelHistory(state, history) in src/mainview/utils/state.ts that re-seeds posts/postOrder from history but carries over already-loaded metadata.reactions from existing state. The sync effect in MainViewApp now uses it, and only calls loadPostReactions for posts that don't already have reactions. Covered by new unit tests in state.test.ts.
